content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
July 22,
1992
Betsy Strauss
City Attorney
1000 Webster Street
Fairfield,
CA
94533
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A-92-395
Dear Ms.
Strauss:
-
This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding the duties and responsibilities of Vice—Mayor Ichikawa,
Councilmember Hammond and Councilmember Pettygrove under the
conflict—of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) •1
QUESTION
The vice—mayor and councilmembers own interests in real
property situated beyond a radius of 300 feet but within 2,500
feet of the Waterman
& Capitola project.
May they participate in
governmental decisions regarding this project if the effect of the
decisions on their interests in real property will not be
material?
FACTS
We have previously provided you with advice regarding the
participation of Vice—Mayor Ichikawa,
Councilmember Hammond and
Councilmember Pettygrove in decisions regarding the Waterman
&
Capitola project.
(Strauss Advice Letter,
No.
1—92—290.)
We concluded,
in our previous letter, that the officials on whose
behalf you seek our advice could participate in decisions
regarding this project as long as the decisions would not have a
material financial effect on their interests in real property.
We
1
Government Code Sections 81000—91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95812 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No. A-92-395
Page
2
further advised you that to obtain written advice from the
Commission which grants immunity from Commission enforcement
action,
a written statement to the Commission declaring that there
will be no material financial effect would be sufficient as long
as the Commission’s materiality regulations have been applied in
reaching this determination.
You have now submitted for our consideration a report,
prepared by an appraiser, that states that the pending decisions
will not have a measurable effect on the officials’ interests in
real property.
You have also submitted for our review a statement
of the qualifications of the appraiser.
CONCLUSION
To the extent that the appraiser was qualified to make the
determination that decisions regarding the Waterman
& Capitola
project will not affect the officials’ interests in real property
materially,
and to the extent that the factors in Regulation
18702.3(d)
have been taken
into ‘consideration to reach this
determination, the officials are not disqualified from
participating in these decisions.
2
We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter’,
do
not hesitate to call me at
(916)
322—59Ol.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
By:
Blanca M.
Breeze
Counsel,
Legal Division
SH: BMB: bb
2
As we have previously advised you, the Commission does not act
as a finder of fact when it issues advice.
(In re Ocilesby
(1975)
1 FPPC Ops. 71,77.)
Accordingly, our advice is limited to the
facts as presented for our review.
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10 per page.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
